schools.summary: Moses gathering the congregation to declare God's commands: 'and Moses gathered all the congregation of the children of Israel together, and said unto them, These are the words which the LORD hath commanded, that ye should do them' — Moses gathering the whole congregation to declare the commands of the LORD (concerning the Sabbath and the building of the tabernacle, chs. 25-31), that they should do them (the assembly, the whole congregation gathered, after the renewal of the covenant, ch. 34, the declaring, Moses faithfully delivering God's commands, the doing, the commands given to be obeyed); Moses declaring the LORD's words (the commands concerning the tabernacle and the Sabbath) — the faithful delivery (Moses declaring all God's word, the obedience, the commands to be done); 'that ye should do them' (the commands to be obeyed, the doing, not the hearing only, James 1:22, the providence, the renewed people set to the work of God's house, the obedience of faith after the renewal of the covenant) — Henry, Gill.

themes: And Moses gathered all the congregation of the children of Israel together, and said unto them, These are the words which the LORD hath commanded, that ye should do them
Moses gathering the congregation to declare God's commands
Moses faithfully delivering all God's word to the renewed people
the commands to be obeyed, doers not hearers only (James 1:22)

schools.summary: The Sabbath commanded before the work of the sanctuary: 'six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you an holy day, a sabbath of rest to the LORD: whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death' — the Sabbath commanded first (before the building of the tabernacle, chs. 35-40), the seventh day a holy rest to the LORD, on pain of death for working (the Sabbath, the weekly rest, the fourth commandment, Exodus 20:8-11; 31:13-17, the death penalty, the strictness of the Sabbath rest); the order — the Sabbath commanded before the work of the tabernacle (the witnesses noting the lesson — the work of God's house, however holy, was not to break the Sabbath, the building of the sanctuary, the most sacred work, must yield to the Sabbath rest, the priority of the Sabbath, the rest of God not to be set aside even for the work of building his house, the holiness of the day, the Sabbath holy, the rest sacred, the figure of the rest in God, Hebrews 4:9-10, the rest before the work, the worship before the labor, the lesson — God to be served in his own way, the Sabbath kept even amid the holiest service); 'whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death' (the death penalty, the strictness of the Sabbath, the sacredness of the rest, Exodus 31:14-15, the holiness of the day, the providence, the Sabbath guarded, the rest sacred, even the work of the tabernacle yielding to it) — Keil and Delitzsch, Wesley, JFB, Gill.

debates.question: Why is the Sabbath commanded before the work of the tabernacle?

sides.position: The most sacred work (building God's house) was not to break the Sabbath rest (Exodus 20:8-11; 31:13-17) - the priority of God's rest over even the holiest labor, the holiness of the day (a figure of the rest in God, Hebrews 4:9-10), the worship and rest before the work

themes: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you an holy day, a sabbath of rest to the LORD: whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death
the Sabbath commanded before the work of the sanctuary (the crux of the rest before the work)
even the most sacred work (building God's house) not to break the Sabbath (Exodus 20:8-11; 31:13-17)
the priority of God's rest, a figure of the rest in God (Hebrews 4:9-10)

schools.summary: The prohibition of kindling fire on the Sabbath: 'ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day' — no fire to be kindled in the homes on the Sabbath (for work, cooking, or labor, the prohibition, the strictness of the Sabbath rest, even the kindling of fire forbidden, the rest, the complete cessation from labor, the homes, throughout all their habitations, the rest universal); the prohibition of fire (the kindling for work or cooking forbidden, the food prepared the day before, Exodus 16:23) — the strictness (the Sabbath rest complete, the lesson, the cessation from all servile labor, the Jews variously applied this, some too strictly, the principle, the rest from labor, the day kept holy); 'throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day' (the rest in every home, the Sabbath universal, the providence, the Sabbath kept in every house, the rest sacred, the figure of the rest from our works in Christ, Hebrews 4:10) — Clarke, Gill, Wesley.

themes: Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day
the prohibition of kindling fire on the Sabbath
the complete cessation from labor, the Sabbath rest strict (Exodus 16:23)
the Sabbath kept holy in every home, a figure of the rest in Christ (Hebrews 4:10)

schools.summary: Moses introducing the command for the offering: 'and Moses spake unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the LORD commanded, saying' — Moses introducing the command of the LORD concerning the offering for the tabernacle (vv.5-9, the introduction, Moses declaring God's command for the freewill offering, the authority, the command the LORD's, not Moses' invention); Moses declaring the LORD's command (the offering for the tabernacle) — the divine authority (the command from God); Moses introducing the command for the offering (vv.5-9) — the freewill offering for the building of the sanctuary (the providence, the people called to give for God's house, the offering of the willing heart) — Gill, Henry.

themes: And Moses spake unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the LORD commanded, saying
Moses introducing the command for the offering
Moses declaring God's command for the freewill offering
the divine authority, the command from God (vv.5-9)

schools.summary: The freewill offering of the willing heart: 'take ye from among you an offering unto the LORD: whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the LORD; gold, and silver, and brass' — the call — 'whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the LORD': the people called to bring an offering for the tabernacle, from a willing heart (not by compulsion, but freely, cheerfully, as Exodus 25:2, the witnesses noting the principle — the freewill offering, given from a willing heart, the acceptable giving, the cheerful giver, 2 Corinthians 9:7, 'every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver,' the lesson — the spirit of acceptable giving, the willing heart, not the amount, the heart the measure, 2 Corinthians 8:12, the gladness of giving to God's house, the people's hearts stirred to give freely, vv.21-29; 1 Chronicles 29:9, 14, 'the people rejoiced, for that they offered willingly... of thine own have we given thee,' the freewill offering for the sanctuary); the materials (gold, silver, brass, and the rest, vv.6-9) — the offering (the precious materials for the tabernacle, given freely, the figure, the first and best given to God); the willing heart bringing the offering — the freewill gift (the providence, the people giving freely for God's house, the willing heart the measure, the figure of the cheerful giver, 2 Corinthians 9:7) — Calvin, Gill.

debates.question: What is the measure of acceptable giving?

sides.position: The willing, cheerful heart, not the amount - the freewill offering given freely, not by compulsion (2 Corinthians 9:7, 'God loveth a cheerful giver'; 8:12; Exodus 25:2); the gladness of giving to God's house (1 Chronicles 29:9, 14)

themes: Take ye from among you an offering unto the LORD: whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the LORD; gold, and silver, and brass
the freewill offering of the willing heart (the crux of willing giving)
the freewill offering given from a willing, cheerful heart, not by compulsion (2 Corinthians 9:7; 8:12; Exodus 25:2)
the heart the measure of acceptable giving, the gladness of giving to God (1 Chronicles 29:9, 14)

schools.summary: The skins and wood of the offering: 'and rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood' — among the materials brought for the tabernacle were rams' skins dyed red, badgers' skins (durable leather), and shittim wood (acacia, the coverings, the skins for the outer coverings of the tent, and the wood, the acacia for the framework, the boards, the furniture, the materials, the durable, plain materials beneath the rich fabrics); the skins (the outer coverings of the tent) — the protection (the figure, the lowly outward covering veiling the inner glory, as Christ, Isaiah 53:2); 'and shittim wood' (the acacia, the incorruptible desert wood, for the framework and furniture, the figure, the incorruptible wood overlaid with gold, a figure of the humanity of Christ joined with his deity, the providence, the materials for the tabernacle, freely given) — Clarke.

themes: And rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood
the skins and wood of the offering
the durable coverings and incorruptible acacia for the tabernacle
figures of the lowly covering and the humanity of Christ (Isaiah 53:2)

schools.summary: The oil, spices, and incense of the offering: 'and oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense' — among the materials were oil for the lamps (the light of the candlestick), spices for the holy anointing oil (Exodus 30:23-25), and for the sweet incense (Exodus 30:34-38, the materials of worship, the oil for the light, the anointing for consecration, the incense for prayer, the figures, the oil of the Spirit, the anointing, the incense of prayer); the oil for the lamps (the light of the sanctuary) — the figure (the oil of the Spirit, the light of testimony, Zechariah 4:2-6; Matthew 5:14-16); 'spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense' (the anointing oil, the figure of the Spirit's anointing, 1 John 2:20, 27, and the sweet incense, the figure of prayer, Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8, the providence, the materials of worship, figures of the Spirit, the anointing, and prayer) — JFB, Clarke.

themes: And oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense
the oil, spices, and incense of the offering
figures of the Spirit's light and anointing (Zechariah 4:2-6; 1 John 2:20)
the incense of prayer (Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8)

schools.summary: The precious stones of the offering: 'and onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate' — among the materials were onyx stones (for the high priest's shoulder-pieces, Exodus 28:9-12) and the precious stones for the breastplate (the twelve tribes, Exodus 28:17-21, the stones for the high priest's vestments, the figure, the high priest bearing the tribes on his shoulders and heart, a figure of Christ bearing his people); the onyx stones (the two on the high priest's shoulders, engraved with the tribes' names, Exodus 28:9-12) — the bearing of the tribes (the high priest bearing the people, a figure of Christ); 'stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate' (the twelve stones of the breastplate, the tribes borne on the heart, Exodus 28:17-29, the figure, the high priest bearing the tribes on shoulders and heart, a type of Christ bearing his people, Isaiah 49:16, the providence, the precious stones for the garments, freely given) — Clarke.

themes: And onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate
the precious stones of the offering
the tribes borne on the high priest's shoulders and heart (Exodus 28:9-12, 17-29)
a figure of Christ bearing his people (Isaiah 49:16)

schools.summary: The call for the wise-hearted to make the tabernacle: 'and every wise hearted among you shall come, and make all that the LORD hath commanded' — the wise-hearted (the skilled, gifted workmen) called to come and make all that God commanded for the tabernacle (the call, the skilled invited to the work, the wise-hearted, the skill a gift of God, the craftsmanship divinely given, vv.30-35, the work, all as God commanded, the tabernacle and its furniture); the wise-hearted (the skilled, whose skill is God's gift) — the gift (the craftsmanship from God, the Spirit-given skill, vv.31; James 1:17, the lesson, all ability God's gift, the work of God's house by his gifts); 'make all that the LORD hath commanded' (the work according to God's command, all as appointed, the pattern, Exodus 25:40, the providence, the skilled doing the work of God's house, the gifts employed for God, the figure of the whole people gifted for the work of God's house, 1 Corinthians 12) — Calvin, Gill.

themes: And every wise hearted among you shall come, and make all that the LORD hath commanded
the call for the wise-hearted to make the tabernacle
the skill and craftsmanship a gift of God (James 1:17; vv.30-35)
the work of God's house done by his gifts, all as commanded (Exodus 25:40)

schools.summary: The structure of the tabernacle to be made: 'the tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets' — the list beginning with the structure of the tabernacle: the tent (the curtains), the covering (the skins), the taches (clasps), the boards, bars, pillars, and sockets (Exodus 26, the structure, the framework and coverings of the dwelling, the parts, each part of the tabernacle to be made); the tent and covering (the curtains and skins of the tabernacle, Exodus 26) — the structure (the dwelling-place); 'his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets' (the framework, the clasps, boards, bars, pillars, sockets, Exodus 26, the providence, the structure of God's dwelling, every part to be made, the figure of the church built up, every part needful, Ephesians 2:21-22) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: The tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets
the structure of the tabernacle to be made
the framework and coverings of God's dwelling (Exodus 26)
the figure of the church built up, every part needful (Ephesians 2:21-22)

schools.summary: The ark, mercy seat, and veil to be made: 'the ark, and the staves thereof, with the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering' — the list continuing with the holiest furniture: the ark (with its staves), the mercy seat, and the veil (Exodus 25-26, the ark, the throne of God, the chest for the law, the mercy seat, the propitiatory, the place of atonement and meeting, the veil, dividing the holy from the most holy); the ark and mercy seat (the holiest furniture, the throne of God, the place of propitiation, Exodus 25) — the figures (the ark and mercy seat, figures of Christ, the propitiation, Romans 3:25); 'and the vail of the covering' (the veil, dividing the holy from the most holy, Exodus 26:31-33, the figure, the veil a figure of Christ's flesh, the way into the holiest, Hebrews 10:19-20, the providence, the holiest furniture to be made, the figures of Christ and the access to God) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: The ark, and the staves thereof, with the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering
the ark, mercy seat, and veil to be made
the throne of God, the propitiation, the veil (Exodus 25-26)
figures of Christ and the access to God (Romans 3:25; Hebrews 10:19-20)

schools.summary: The table and shewbread to be made: 'the table, and his staves, and all his vessels, and the shewbread' — the list continuing with the table of shewbread (with its staves and vessels) and the shewbread itself (Exodus 25:23-30, the table, the table of the bread of the Presence, in the holy place, the shewbread, the twelve loaves, the bread before God, the figure of Christ the bread of life, John 6:35); the table and its vessels (the furniture for the shewbread, Exodus 25:23-29) — the table of the LORD (the figure, the table of the bread of the Presence); 'and the shewbread' (the shewbread, the bread of the Presence, the twelve tribes before God, the figure of Christ the bread of life, John 6:35, and the communion, 1 Corinthians 10:17, the providence, the table and bread, the figure of Christ and his people before God) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: The table, and his staves, and all his vessels, and the shewbread
the table and shewbread to be made
the table of the LORD and the bread before God (Exodus 25:23-30)
the figure of Christ the bread of life (John 6:35; 1 Corinthians 10:17)

schools.summary: The candlestick and the light to be made: 'the candlestick also for the light, and his furniture, and his lamps, with the oil for the light' — the list continuing with the golden candlestick (with its furniture, lamps, and the oil for the light, Exodus 25:31-40, the candlestick, the seven-branched lampstand, the light of the holy place, the lamps and oil, the light burning continually, the figure, the light of the Spirit, the church the light-bearer, Revelation 1:20, the light of testimony); the candlestick (the lampstand, the light of the sanctuary, Exodus 25:31) — the figure (the church the light-bearer, or Christ the light, John 8:12); 'his lamps, with the oil for the light' (the lamps and oil, the light burning, the oil of the Spirit feeding the light, Zechariah 4:2-6, the figure, the light of testimony, the church the light of the world, Matthew 5:14-16, the providence, the candlestick and light to be made, the figure of the Spirit and the church) — JFB, Gill.

themes: The candlestick also for the light, and his furniture, and his lamps, with the oil for the light
the candlestick and the light to be made
the light of the holy place, the lamps fed by the oil (Exodus 25:31-40)
the figure of the Spirit's light and the church the light-bearer (Zechariah 4:2-6; Matthew 5:14-16)

schools.summary: The incense altar, oil, and the door hanging to be made: 'and the incense altar, and his staves, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle' — the list continuing with the golden altar of incense (Exodus 30:1-10), the holy anointing oil and sweet incense (Exodus 30:22-38), and the hanging (screen) for the door of the tabernacle (Exodus 26:36, the altar of incense, the place of prayer, the oil and incense, the Spirit's anointing and prayer, the door hanging, the entrance to the holy place); the incense altar (the golden altar of prayer, Exodus 30:1) — the figure (prayer ascending to God, Revelation 8:3-4); 'the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle' (the oil, the Spirit's anointing, the incense, prayer, and the door hanging, the entrance, Exodus 26:36, the providence, the items of worship and the entrance to be made) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And the incense altar, and his staves, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle
the incense altar, oil, and the door hanging to be made
the altar of prayer, the Spirit's anointing, the incense of prayer (Exodus 30; Revelation 8:3-4)
the entrance to the holy place (Exodus 26:36)

schools.summary: The altar of burnt offering and the laver to be made: 'the altar of burnt offering, with his brasen grate, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot' — the list continuing with the brazen altar of burnt offering (with its grate, staves, and vessels, Exodus 27:1-8) and the laver (with its base, Exodus 30:18-21, the altar, the place of sacrifice and atonement, a figure of the cross, the laver, the place of washing, a figure of cleansing); the altar of burnt offering (the brazen altar, the place of sacrifice, Exodus 27:1) — the figure (the cross, the place of atonement, Hebrews 13:10); 'the laver and his foot' (the laver, the place of washing, Exodus 30:18, the figure, the washing of regeneration and sanctification, Titus 3:5; Ephesians 5:26, the providence, the altar and laver, figures of the cross and the cleansing) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: The altar of burnt offering, with his brasen grate, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot
the altar of burnt offering and the laver to be made
the place of sacrifice and atonement (a figure of the cross, Hebrews 13:10)
the place of washing (a figure of cleansing, Titus 3:5; Ephesians 5:26)

schools.summary: The hangings of the court to be made: 'the hangings of the court, his pillars, and their sockets, and the hanging for the door of the court' — the list continuing with the hangings of the court (the white linen walls), the pillars and sockets, and the hanging (gate) for the door of the court (Exodus 27:9-19, the court, the enclosure around the tabernacle, the hangings, the white linen walls, the figure of purity, the gate, the one entrance, a figure of Christ the door, John 10:9); the hangings (the white linen walls of the court, Exodus 27:9) — the figure (the purity, the separation of God's house, Revelation 19:8); 'and the hanging for the door of the court' (the gate, the one entrance into the court, Exodus 27:16, the figure, the one door, Christ the way, John 10:9; 14:6, the providence, the court and gate to be made, the figure of the enclosure and the one entrance) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: The hangings of the court, his pillars, and their sockets, and the hanging for the door of the court
the hangings of the court to be made
the white linen walls enclosing God's house, the figure of purity (Revelation 19:8)
the one gate, a figure of Christ the door (John 10:9; 14:6)

schools.summary: The pins and cords of the tabernacle: 'the pins of the tabernacle, and the pins of the court, and their cords' — the list including the pins (tent-pegs, stakes) of the tabernacle and the court, and their cords (ropes, the pins and cords, the small but necessary parts, holding the curtains and hangings firm against the wind, Exodus 27:19, the smallest parts, the pins and cords needful to hold the structure together); the pins (the tent-pegs, holding the structure firm) — the figure (the small but necessary parts, every member needful, 1 Corinthians 12:22, 'those members... which seem to be more feeble, are necessary'); 'and their cords' (the cords, the ropes, holding the curtains and hangings, the figure, the small things that hold God's house together, every part needful, the providence, the pins and cords to be made, the least parts of God's house, the figure of every member's place in the church) — Gill.

themes: The pins of the tabernacle, and the pins of the court, and their cords
the pins and cords of the tabernacle
the small but necessary parts holding the structure firm (Exodus 27:19)
the figure of every member's needful place (1 Corinthians 12:22)

schools.summary: The priestly garments to be made: 'the cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office' — the list concluding with the cloths of service (the woven garments for the ministry) and the holy garments for Aaron and his sons (the priestly vestments, Exodus 28, the garments, the holy vestments for the priesthood, the ministry, the priests clothed for their service); the holy garments for Aaron (the high priest's vestments, the ephod, breastplate, robe, mitre, Exodus 28) — the figure (the garments of glory and beauty, the figure of Christ the great High Priest, Hebrews 2:9; Exodus 28:2); 'the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office' (the garments of the common priests, Exodus 28:40, the figure, the priestly people clothed for service, a royal priesthood, 1 Peter 2:9, the providence, the garments for the ministry, the figure of Christ the High Priest and his priestly people) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: The cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office
the priestly garments to be made
the holy vestments for the priesthood (Exodus 28)
the figure of Christ the great High Priest and his priestly people (Hebrews 2:9; 1 Peter 2:9)

schools.summary: The congregation departing to bring their offerings: 'and all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses' — the people, having heard the command (vv.4-19), departing from Moses to fetch their offerings (vv.21-29, the departure, the people going to their tents to bring their gifts, the response, the people stirred to give, going to fetch their offerings); the people departing (to bring their offerings, the prompt response) — the willingness (the people eager to give, going to fetch their gifts); the congregation departing to bring their offerings (vv.21-29) — the prompt, willing response (the providence, the people stirred to give, the overflowing offering, vv.21-29) — Henry, Gill, Calvin.

themes: And all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses
the congregation departing to bring their offerings
the prompt, willing response to the command (vv.4-19)
the people stirred to give, going to fetch their gifts (vv.21-29)

schools.summary: The willing-hearted bringing the offering: 'and they came, every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing, and they brought the LORD'S offering to the work of the tabernacle of the congregation, and for all his service, and for the holy garments' — the willing spirit — 'every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing': the people bringing their offerings, every one whose heart was stirred and whose spirit made him willing (the witnesses noting the meaning — the freewill offering flowing from a willing, stirred heart, not by compulsion, but the heart moved, the spirit made willing, the lesson — the willing heart the source of acceptable giving, the heart stirred by God, the spirit made willing, the inward prompting to give freely, 2 Corinthians 9:7; 8:12, the people's hearts moved to give for God's house, as 1 Chronicles 29:9, 14, 'the people rejoiced, for that they offered willingly... with perfect heart,' the work of God's house done by willing hearts, the figure of the cheerful, Spirit-prompted giving, the heart stirred and made willing by God, the inward grace prompting the outward gift); the offering brought for the work of the tabernacle (the gifts for God's house) — the willing giving (the people bringing freely, the offering for the work, the service, and the garments); every one whose heart stirred him up — the willing heart the measure (the providence, the people's hearts stirred to give, the overflowing willing offering, vv.22-29, the figure of the willing, Spirit-prompted giving) — Wesley, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does 'whose heart stirred him up, and whose spirit made him willing' teach?

sides.position: The freewill offering flowing from a stirred, willing heart (2 Corinthians 9:7; 8:12) - the willing heart the source of acceptable giving, the heart moved and the spirit made willing by God (1 Chronicles 29:9, 14), the work of God's house done by willing, Spirit-prompted hearts

themes: And they came, every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing, and they brought the LORD'S offering to the work of the tabernacle
the willing-hearted bringing the offering (the crux of the willing spirit)
the freewill offering flowing from a stirred, willing heart (2 Corinthians 9:7; 8:12)
the heart moved and spirit made willing by God, the work of God's house by willing hearts (1 Chronicles 29:9, 14)

schools.summary: The men and women bringing their gold willingly: 'and they came, both men and women, as many as were willing hearted, and brought bracelets, and earrings, and rings, and tablets, all jewels of gold: and every man that offered offered an offering of gold unto the LORD' — both men and women, all the willing-hearted, bringing their gold jewelry (bracelets, earrings, rings, tablets) as an offering (the willing-hearted, the eager givers, men and women alike, the gold, the jewels freely given for God's house); the men and women (all the willing, the whole people, even the women, giving) — the universal giving (the whole congregation, men and women, the willing-hearted, the contrast, the same gold once given for the calf, vv.32:3, now given for God's house); 'every man that offered offered an offering of gold unto the LORD' (the gold offered to the LORD, the freewill gift, the providence, the people giving their gold for God's house, the willing offering, the figure of the consecration of our treasure to God) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott.

themes: And they came, both men and women, as many as were willing hearted, and brought... all jewels of gold: and every man that offered offered an offering of gold unto the LORD
the men and women bringing their gold willingly
the whole people, men and women, the willing-hearted giving
the gold once given for the calf now consecrated to God (Exodus 32:3)

schools.summary: The fabrics and skins brought for the offering: 'and every man, with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair, and red skins of rams, and badgers' skins, brought them' — those who had blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair, and the skins bringing them for the tabernacle (the materials, the rich fabrics and the skins, for the curtains, coverings, and garments, the giving, each bringing what he had, the willing offering); the rich fabrics (the blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair) — the materials (the figures of the heavenly, kingly, sacrificial, and pure, for God's house); 'red skins of rams, and badgers' skins, brought them' (the skins, the coverings of the tent, the giving, each bringing what he had, the providence, the materials freely given, the willing offering for God's house) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And every man, with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair, and red skins of rams, and badgers' skins, brought them
the fabrics and skins brought for the offering
the rich materials and coverings freely given for the tabernacle
each bringing what he had, the willing offering

schools.summary: The silver, brass, and wood brought for the offering: 'every one that did offer an offering of silver and brass brought the LORD'S offering: and every man, with whom was found shittim wood for any work of the service, brought it' — those who had silver, brass, and shittim wood (acacia) bringing them for the work (the metals, silver and brass, for the sockets, the court, the vessels, the wood, the acacia, for the framework and furniture, the giving, each bringing what he had for the work); the silver and brass (the metals for the structure and vessels) — the materials (the metals freely given); 'shittim wood for any work of the service, brought it' (the acacia, the incorruptible wood, for the framework and furniture, the giving, each bringing what he had for the work, the providence, the materials freely given, the willing offering for God's house) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: Every one that did offer an offering of silver and brass brought the LORD'S offering: and every man, with whom was found shittim wood for any work of the service, brought it
the silver, brass, and wood brought for the offering
the metals and wood freely given for the work
each bringing what he had for the work of God's house

schools.summary: The wise-hearted women spinning for the tabernacle: 'and all the women that were wise hearted did spin with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, both of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine linen' — the wise-hearted (skilled) women spinning the yarn (blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen) with their hands for the tabernacle (the women, the skilled women contributing their labor and skill, the spinning, the women's work, their gift of skill for God's house); the wise-hearted women (the skilled, gifted with the craft of spinning) — the gift (the women's skill a gift of God, the craftsmanship for God's house, the lesson, the whole people, men and women, contributing their skill and labor, even the women's spinning a service to God, every gift employed for the work); 'brought that which they had spun' (the women bringing their spun yarn, the fruit of their labor and skill, the providence, the women's gift, the whole people gifted for the work, the figure of every member contributing to God's house, 1 Corinthians 12) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And all the women that were wise hearted did spin with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, both of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine linen
the wise-hearted women spinning for the tabernacle
the skilled women contributing their labor and skill for God's house
every gift employed for the work, every member contributing (1 Corinthians 12)

schools.summary: The women spinning goats' hair: 'and all the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun goats' hair' — the women whose hearts were stirred (and who had the skill, wisdom) spinning the goats' hair (for the coverings of the tent, Exodus 26:7, the women, the willing, skilled women contributing, the goats' hair, for the tent-coverings, the stirred heart, the women moved to use their skill for God's house); the women's hearts stirred (the willingness joined with the skill, the wisdom) — the willing skill (the women moved by their hearts to use their gift, the lesson, the heart and the skill both employed for God); 'spun goats' hair' (the goats' hair spun, for the tent-coverings, the providence, the women's willing labor, the whole people contributing their skill and heart, the figure of every gift and willing heart employed for God's house) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And all the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun goats' hair
the women spinning goats' hair
the willing, skilled women contributing their labor (Exodus 26:7)
the heart and the skill both employed for God's house

schools.summary: The rulers bringing the precious stones: 'and the rulers brought onyx stones, and stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate' — the rulers (the princes, the heads of the tribes) bringing the onyx stones and precious stones for the high priest's ephod and breastplate (Exodus 28:9-21, the rulers, the leaders contributing the costly stones, the stones, the precious stones for the high priest's vestments, the costliest materials); the rulers (the princes) bringing the precious stones (the costliest materials, fitting their station) — the giving (the leaders contributing the precious stones); 'stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate' (the stones for the high priest's vestments, the shoulder-stones and the breastplate, the tribes borne, Exodus 28, the figure, the high priest bearing the tribes, a figure of Christ, the providence, the rulers giving the precious stones, the whole people contributing according to their means) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: And the rulers brought onyx stones, and stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate
the rulers bringing the precious stones
the leaders contributing the costliest materials for the high priest's vestments (Exodus 28:9-21)
the whole people giving according to their means

schools.summary: The spice and oil brought by the rulers: 'and spice, and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense' — the rulers also bringing spice and oil (for the light, the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, Exodus 30, the spice and oil, the materials for the light, the anointing, and the incense, the figures, the oil of the Spirit, the anointing, the incense of prayer, the giving, the leaders contributing the costly spice and oil); the spice and oil (for the light, the anointing, the incense) — the materials of worship (the figures of the Spirit and prayer); 'for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense' (the anointing oil, the Spirit's anointing, and the sweet incense, prayer, Exodus 30:23-38, the providence, the spice and oil for worship, freely given, the whole people contributing) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And spice, and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense
the spice and oil brought by the rulers
the materials of worship freely given (Exodus 30:23-38)
figures of the Spirit's light and anointing and the incense of prayer

schools.summary: The willing offering of all the people: 'the children of Israel brought a willing offering unto the LORD, every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all manner of work, which the LORD had commanded to be made by the hand of Moses' — all the people, men and women, whose hearts made them willing, bringing a willing offering (a freewill gift) for the work of the tabernacle (the willing offering, the freewill gift of the whole people, the universality, every man and woman, the whole congregation, willing-hearted); the willing heart (the source of the offering, the heart made willing) — the freewill giving (the heart the measure, the willing offering acceptable, 2 Corinthians 9:7; 8:12, the lesson, the work of God's house done by the willing offering of the whole people); 'for all manner of work, which the LORD had commanded to be made by the hand of Moses' (the offering for all the work, the whole tabernacle, the providence, the willing offering supplying all the work, the people giving more than enough, Exodus 36:5-7, the figure of the willing, abundant giving for God's house) — Gill.

themes: The children of Israel brought a willing offering unto the LORD, every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all manner of work
the willing offering of all the people
the whole people, men and women, giving freely for God's house (2 Corinthians 9:7; 8:12)
the willing offering supplying all the work, more than enough (Exodus 36:5-7)

schools.summary: Bezaleel called by name to the work: 'and Moses said unto the children of Israel, See, the LORD hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah' — God calling Bezaleel by name (specifically chosen, Exodus 31:2) for the work of the tabernacle (the calling by name, the particular, personal choice of God, the tribe of Judah, the royal tribe, the tribe of Christ, the chief workman, Bezaleel the master craftsman, gifted for the work); God calling Bezaleel by name, and (vv.31) filling him with the Spirit of God for the work (the lesson — the workman for God's house called and gifted by God, the calling and the gift both from God, the skill a gift of the Spirit, vv.31, the work of God's house done by God-given, Spirit-filled men, the particular calling, God calling his servants by name to their work, Isaiah 45:4; John 10:3, the figure, the Spirit's gifts for the building of God's house, the figure of the gifts for the building of the church, 1 Corinthians 12; Ephesians 4:11-12); Bezaleel of Judah (the royal tribe) — the providence (the chief workman of Judah, the tribe of Christ, the gifts of God for his house) — Henry, Calvin, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the calling of Bezaleel by name signify?

sides.position: The workman for God's house called and gifted by God (Exodus 31:2), filled with the Spirit for the work (vv.31) - the calling and the gift both from God, a figure of the Spirit's gifts for the building of God's house and his church (1 Corinthians 12; Ephesians 4:11-12); God calling his servants by name to their work (Isaiah 45:4; John 10:3)

themes: And Moses said unto the children of Israel, See, the LORD hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah
Bezaleel called by name to the work (the crux of the gifted workman)
the workman for God's house called and gifted by God (Exodus 31:2; Isaiah 45:4)
a figure of the Spirit's gifts for the building of God's house and church (1 Corinthians 12; Ephesians 4:11-12)

schools.summary: Bezaleel filled with the Spirit for the work: 'and he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship' — God filling Bezaleel with the Spirit of God, giving him wisdom, understanding, knowledge, and skill in all manner of workmanship (the filling with the Spirit, the skill and craftsmanship a gift of the Spirit of God, the wisdom, understanding, knowledge, the threefold gift, the comprehensive skill, the workmanship, the practical skill for the work); the filling with the Spirit — the skill and craftsmanship the gift of the Spirit of God (the witnesses noting the lesson — all skill and ability, even in the arts and crafts, a gift of God's Spirit, the Spirit the source of every gift, not only of grace but of skill, James 1:17; 1 Corinthians 12:4-11, 'diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit,' the work of God's house done by Spirit-given skill, the craftsmen filled with the Spirit for the work, as the ministers gifted for the building of the church, Ephesians 4:11-12, the consecration of skill to God, the gift of skill employed for God's house); 'in all manner of workmanship' (the practical skill, the comprehensive craftsmanship, the artistry, vv.32-33, the providence, the Spirit-filled workman, the figure of the gifts for God's house and church) — Keil and Delitzsch, Calvin, Gill.

debates.question: What does the filling of Bezaleel with the Spirit teach?

sides.position: The skill and craftsmanship the gift of the Spirit of God - all ability even in the arts a gift of the Spirit (James 1:17; 1 Corinthians 12:4-11), the work of God's house done by Spirit-given skill, a figure of the gifts for the building of the church (Ephesians 4:11-12)

themes: And he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship
Bezaleel filled with the Spirit for the work
the skill and craftsmanship the gift of the Spirit of God (James 1:17; 1 Corinthians 12:4-11)
the work of God's house done by Spirit-given skill, a figure of the gifts for the church (Ephesians 4:11-12)

schools.summary: The gift of teaching given to Bezaleel and Aholiab: 'and he hath put in his heart that he may teach, both he, and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan' — God putting in the heart of Bezaleel (and Aholiab) the gift of teaching (to instruct others in the work, the teaching, the gift to teach and direct the other workmen, Aholiab, the second master workman, of the tribe of Dan, vv.31:6, the providence, the gift of teaching, that the skill might be shared, the work carried on by many); the gift of teaching (put in the heart by God, the ability to instruct others) — the gift (the teaching a gift of God, that the skill might be communicated to others, the lesson, the gifts of God to be shared, the skilled to teach others, that the work of God's house be done by many); 'both he, and Aholiab... of the tribe of Dan' (Bezaleel, of Judah, and Aholiab, of Dan, the two master workmen, the chief and his helper, from two tribes, the providence, the workmen of two tribes, the gift of teaching shared, the figure of the gifts for God's house given and communicated, the teachers in the church, Ephesians 4:11-12) — Gill.

themes: And he hath put in his heart that he may teach, both he, and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan
the gift of teaching given to Bezaleel and Aholiab
the gift of teaching, that the skill might be communicated to others (Exodus 31:6)
the figure of the gifts for God's house shared, the teachers in the church (Ephesians 4:11-12)

schools.summary: The workmen filled with skill for every craft: 'them hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work, of the engraver, and of the cunning workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, and in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of them that do any work, and of those that devise cunning work' — God filling the workmen with wisdom of heart for every craft: engraving, cunning (skilled, artistic) work, embroidery (in the rich colors and fine linen), weaving, and all manner of work and design (the filling, the skill the gift of God, for every craft, the crafts, the comprehensive skill, every kind of work for the tabernacle); the wisdom of heart (the skill, the gift of God, for all manner of work) — the gift (the comprehensive skill, every craft a gift of the Spirit, vv.31, the lesson, all the crafts and arts gifts of God, the various gifts for the various works of God's house); 'of the engraver... the cunning workman... the embroiderer... the weaver... those that devise cunning work' (the various crafts, engraving, design, embroidery, weaving, devising, the figure, the variety of gifts for the one work, the diversity of gifts in the one Spirit, 1 Corinthians 12:4-11, the various gifts for the building of God's house and church, the providence, the workmen gifted for every craft, the whole work of God's house done by the variety of God-given gifts) — Gill.

themes: Them hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work, of the engraver, and of the cunning workman, and of the embroiderer... and of the weaver... and of those that devise cunning work
the workmen filled with skill for every craft
the various crafts, every kind of work for the tabernacle
the variety of gifts for the one work, the diversity of gifts in the one Spirit (1 Corinthians 12:4-11)
